title: ESPN reporter explains why June 1st could be a big day for teams looking to trade for A.J. Brown
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: Trading A.J. Brown before June 1 would cost the Eagles $43.5 million in dead cap, versus $16.353 million after.
The Patriots might target A.J. Brown for wide receiver depth, having lost Stefon Diggs and added Romeo Doubs this offseason.
The Eagles’ asking price for Brown may resemble a Quinnen Williams-type deal, involving significant draft picks and a player.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her mother’s fight against cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Allyson Bogdanski, 45, from Medford, Mass., is running the Boston Marathon to honor her mother's battle with breast cancer.
Her mother, a two-time survivor, received treatment at Dana-Farber, where she continues to receive care and support.
Bogdanski aims to celebrate her mother's resilience and the dedication of Dana-Farber's medical team through the marathon.

title: GoFundMe for Boston officer charged in fatal shooting soars past $400K
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Officer Nicholas O'Malley has been suspended after being charged with manslaughter in the shooting of Stephenson King.
A GoFundMe page for O'Malley's family has raised over $407,000, supported by fellow officers and Boston City Councilor Ed Flynn.
O'Malley pleaded not guilty to manslaughter charges and is backed by some city councilors amid calls for transparency in the case.

title: AI attack ads are hitting Massachusetts political campaigns
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Massachusetts lawmakers consider legislation to regulate AI in political ads due to concerns over misinformation and voter influence.
Republican Marc Lombardo used AI in a March 11 attack ad against his opponent in the Billerica House race.
Massachusetts House passed bills H.5093 and H.5094 to address AI-generated election misinformation and require AI content disclosure.

title: GoFundMe launched for Mass. family after teen dies in crash from overpass
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Travis Teixeira, 18, of Ashburnham, died after his car fell from a Route 2 overpass in Leominster.
A GoFundMe campaign has raised over $70,000 for Teixeira's family to cover funeral expenses and other costs.
The fundraiser was organized by a relative to support the family during their time of loss and grief.

title: ‘Stay strong’: David Ortiz’s defiant declaration in the wake of tragedy still resonates with Boston
author: Chad Finn
bullets: David Ortiz addressed Fenway Park on April 20, 2013, after the Boston Marathon bombings, delivering a memorable speech.
Ortiz thanked local officials and police, declaring 'This is our [expletive] city' in defense of Boston's resilience.
The FCC allowed Ortiz's curse during the speech, recognizing its significance in uplifting a devastated city.

title: Holy Cross student missing after canoe capsizes in Connecticut
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Holy Cross student Dominick Tucci, 20, is missing after a canoe capsized on Lake Hayward in East Haddam, Connecticut.
Search efforts for Tucci involve water-based crews, aerial surveillance, and dive teams, and are expected to continue.
Holy Cross provides counseling to students, faculty, and staff while collaborating with law enforcement in search efforts.

title: The unsolved murder of Kendric Price, a beloved youth mentor gunned down on his childhood street in Dorchester
author: Emily Sweeney, The Boston Globe
bullets: Kendric Price, a youth mentor, was killed in March 2019 on his childhood street in Dorchester, Boston.
Price was involved in mentoring through basketball and founded a nonprofit to teach kids about finances.
The Boston Police Department continues to investigate the unsolved murder, urging anyone with information to come forward.

title: The basement of this $4.7m Lynnfield home known for its holiday decor is like a private club
author: Megan Johnson
bullets: Lynnfield's 1 Haywood Farm home is listed for $4.7 million and features a unique private club-like basement.
The basement includes an eight-person bar, private screening room, kids' playroom, wine tasting room, and sauna.
Known for extravagant holiday decor, the property attracts thousands during Halloween with in-house crafted displays.

title: Patriots reportedly set to release QB Joshua Dobbs
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are reportedly set to release 31-year-old quarterback Joshua Dobbs, according to NFL Network's Ian Rapoport.
Dobbs, who was Drake Maye's backup in 2025, completed 7 out of 10 passes for 65 yards in four games.
Tommy DeVito's recent two-year contract with guaranteed money may have influenced the decision to release Dobbs.
